title: First blog idea
text:
Both Python and R!
Here is an example on how to use python and R in the same script useing the packkage reticulate. First, we need to load the reticulate package. Then we call py_install to install the python libraries we need.





Code
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

r = np.arange(0, 2, 0.01)
theta = 2 * np.pi * r
fig, ax = plt.subplots(
  subplot_kw = {'projection': 'polar'} 
)
ax.plot(theta, r)
ax.set_rticks([0.5, 1, 1.5, 2])
ax.grid(True)
plt.show()





Figure 1: A line plot on a polar axis

title: First Post!
text:
This is a a density plot of 100 samples random normal distribution vs the density function of a normal distribution:

library(ggplot2)
ggplot(data.frame(x = rnorm(1e3, 0, 1)), aes(x = x)) +
    geom_density(color = "#EE6A24", fill = "#170C3A", alpha = .5) +
    theme_minimal() +
    stat_function(fun = dnorm, args = list(mean = 0, sd = 1), fill = "#170C3A", geom = "area", alpha = .5)
